description_long: This gatehouse features a robust, arched stone structure with a central passageway flanked by two solid square pillars. Atop each pillar sits a lantern-style lamp, suggesting illumination for nighttime visibility and security. The archway is constructed from dark, textured stone, giving it a sense of permanence and strength, while the entry is secured by a pair of wooden double doors with decorative paneling. The overall design is symmetrical, with the lamps and pillars mirroring each other on either side of the arch. The absence of windows and the presence of heavy doors emphasize security and controlled access. The gatehouse is likely intended as a formal entry point to a private estate, park, or institutional property.
description: A symmetrical, arched stone gatehouse with wooden double doors and lantern-topped pillars, designed for secure and formal entry.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched stone entry, wooden double doors, and both lantern-topped pillars.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the depth of the structure and the profile of one lantern and pillar.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the arch, doors, and lanterns visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the other pillar and lantern in profile.
attribute_entryway_design: arched stone doorway with wooden double doors
